title: Thelypteris uniauriculata
taxonomy: Thelypteris uniauriculata is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or autumn ferns. This species belongs to the genus Thelypteris, which includes a range of fern species found in tropical and subtropical regions. The exact classification of Thelypteris uniauriculata may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a member of the Thelypteris genus. The classification is based on the plant's morphology and phylogenetic relationships with other fern species. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification of Thelypteris uniauriculata.

intro: Thelypteris uniauriculata, also known as the narrow-leaved maiden fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is used in traditional medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris uniauriculata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris uniauriculata is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create tonics and decoctions.
